chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for navigating to network settings within the Windows Control Panel, guiding the user to 'Network and Internet' and then to 'Change adapter settings'.

key_facts: To access network settings, navigate to the Control Panel and select 'Network and Internet'.
Within the 'Network and Sharing Center', click on 'Change adapter settings' to modify network adapter configurations.

1.6. Set the IP address, Right Click Local Area Connection and select Properties.

specifications.Description: ASIX AX88772B USB2.0 to Fast Ethernet

key_facts: To find your IP address, right-click the Ethernet icon and select 'Status' from the context menu in the 'Network Connections' window.
The 'Network Connection Details' window displays various properties including physical address, IPv4 address, IPv4 subnet mask, and IPv4 default gateway.
The example network connection uses an ASIX AX88772B USB2.0 to Fast Ethernet adapter.
DHCP is currently disabled for the example network connection.
The next step to set the IP address involves right-clicking 'Local Area Connection' (or Ethernet) and selecting 'Properties'.

key_facts: The document illustrates how to navigate to Windows 'Network Connections' to access adapter properties.
It demonstrates right-clicking 'Ethernet' and selecting 'Properties' from the context menu.
The page guides the user to double-click 'Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)' within the 'Ethernet Properties' dialog.
Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol is described as the default wide area network protocol providing communication across diverse interconnected networks.

File and Printer Sharing for Microsoft Networks

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for configuring static IP address settings for a device, including IP address, Subnet mask, and Default gateway, within the Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4) Properties.
chunks.extracted_text:
1.8. Select “Use the Following IP address:” and type in the IP address, Subnet mask and Default gateway. Click OK to apply the settings.

Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4) Properties
General
You can get IP settings assigned automatically if your network supports this capability. Otherwise, you need to ask your network administrator for the appropriate IP settings.
Obtain an IP address automatically
Use the following IP address:
IP address: 192.168.62.17
Subnet mask: 255.255.255.0
Default gateway: 192.168.62.17
Obtain DNS server address automatically
Use the following DNS server addresses:
Preferred DNS server:
Alternate DNS server:
Validate settings upon exit
Advanced...
OK
Cancel

Figure 8. Setting Ip Address.

2. Configuring the static IP address in the SME

2.1. Open RCP and select “Communication” Tab. The following steps should be done with USB communication in the “Setting tab”
key_facts: To manually configure network settings, users must select 'Use the following IP address' and input the IP address, Subnet mask, and Default gateway.
The static IP address for the device is specified as 192.168.62.17, with a Subnet mask of 255.255.255.0 and a Default gateway of 192.168.62.17.
DNS server addresses can also be configured manually, if required.
Configuring the static IP address in the SME involves opening the RCP software and navigating to the 'Communication' tab, performing these steps via USB communication in the 'Setting tab'.
diagram_labels: Use the following DNS server addresses:
warnings_notes: If your network does not support automatic IP address assignment, consult your network administrator for the appropriate IP settings.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is version 5.3.1.6.
The communication settings allow configuration for Modbus RTU, Ethernet, and Bluetooth LTE 4.0.
The LCD display, applicable to SME-TRD products, can be configured to show Viscosity, Density, and Temperature.
Configuration changes can be saved ('Write Config') or loaded ('Load Config') using the panel.
The operation history section tracks real-time clock, last power reset time, supply voltage, and various temperature exposures for the sensor and SME.
The MAC addresses for Ethernet and Bluetooth LTE 4.0 are identical, '70:B3:D5:D2:03:92'.

key_facts: The document illustrates the configuration interface for Rheonics Control Panel software version 5.3.1.6.
It details settings for Modbus RTU, Ethernet, Bluetooth LTE 4.0, and an optional LCD display (SME-TRD Only).
The panel provides operational history, including real-time clock, last power reset, supply voltage, and various temperature exposures.
Users can define units and resolution for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure measurements.
Graphing parameters, including auto/fixed scaling and min/max ranges for different properties, are configurable.
Logging intervals and measurement frequencies can be set through the 'Log Settings' section.
An instruction highlights the process of enabling an IP address within the software for communication setup.
The software indicates communication status with a green LED when correctly configured.
diagram_labels: Log and display data with error code
Plot df (damping) and Fres (resonant frequency) instead of Viscosity & Density
warnings_notes: If everything is correct status led should be green.
